strategy_justification: Direct HTTP (no browser) returns full page with JSON-LD structured data containing all product fields. No JS rendering needed. Anti-bot not detected. This is the simplest, fastest approach.
proxy_justification: Direct connection works perfectly. No proxy needed. HTTP 200 returned with full JSON-LD data.

description.test_result: Elegant bodysuit made of viscose jersey with a natural sheen...

price.note: Numeric value, needs formatting with currency symbol. No highPrice means not on sale.

availability.note: Map schema.org URL to human-readable string. InStock -> 'In Stock', OutOfStock -> 'Out of Stock'

images.note: All images are product images with SKU in URL. Filter by 'assets-cf.armani.com' domain to be safe.

sku.note: JSON-LD has sku='trDwKPAUWA' which is an internal hash, NOT the product code. Extract the real SKU from URL using regex WITHOUT leading '/'. The previous scraper used r'/cod-([A-Z0-9_-]+)' which failed because 'cod-' is mid-segment. FIX: use r'cod-([A-Z0-9_-]+)' (remove leading slash).

color.note: Color text appears in span.text-light-6. Multiple occurrences but all have same value. Take first match. This is available in static HTML — no JS rendering needed.

category.note: BreadcrumbList has 6 positions: Home, GA US, Woman, Clothing, Shirts and Tops, [product_name]. Use positions 3-5 (Woman, Clothing, Shirts and Tops). CRITICAL: EXCLUDE the last item (position 6) which is the product name, not a category. Use items[:-1] then filter position >= 3.

original_price.note: No highPrice in offers means product is NOT on sale. Empty string is correct.

id.note: Same as SKU — extract from URL using the same regex.

location.note: Optional field. Not available for this product. Empty string is correct.

jsonld_structure.format: @graph array with separate Product and BreadcrumbList entries
jsonld_structure.note: JSON-LD uses @graph structure. Must find entries by @type, not root-level keys.

changes_made.description: Previous regex r'/cod-([A-Z0-9_-]+)' failed because it requires '/' immediately before 'cod-', but in the URL the 'cod-' appears mid-segment (after 'bodysuit-'). Fixed to r'cod-([A-Z0-9_-]+)' — remove the leading slash.
BreadcrumbList last item (position 6) is the product name, not a category. Previous code included it, duplicating the title. Fix: exclude last breadcrumb item by using items[:-1] before processing.

retry_adjustments.strategy_reason: HTTP strategy works perfectly. Both failures are code bugs, not connectivity/strategy issues.

escalation_note: No proxy escalation needed. Previous scraper's failures were code bugs (SKU regex and category logic), not connectivity issues. All fields extracted correctly via direct HTTP with JSON-LD.
